title: OOF Sigmoid-Calibrated Regime Blend
summary: You already have several near-tied models, so gain may now come from safer blending.
rationale: You already have several near-tied models, so gain may now come from safer blending. Scikit-learn notes isotonic can change ROC-AUC by introducing ties, while sigmoid is strictly monotonic; for this competition that makes sigmoid the safer post-processing choice.
implementation_hint: Build 2 to 4 diverse specialists such as base classifier, ranker, CatBoost categorical model, and latent-state model. Generate strictly OOF predictions under race-grouped folds, fit a small logistic meta-blender or per-regime sigmoid calibrator on those OOF scores, and only then refit base models on full data for test inference.
expected_effect: More stable leaderboard transfer and a modest lift from complementary errors without sacrificing ranking quality.
risk: Main trap is leakage from in-fold blending/calibration; isotonic may also reduce AUC through tied predictions if overused.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
